notes: Junction wavelengths determined empirically from corpus PCA noise-level analysis. UV/VIS boundary at 365 nm, VIS/NIR boundary at 620 nm.

segment_response_fallback.provenance: Corpus-derived VIS->NIR throughput (gain) step at the 620 nm junction, used when a spectrum's own continuum is too weak to measure it. Median of the continuum step across 435 SciAps Z-300 AverageSpectrum spectra whose continuum exceeded ~20x noise on both flanks (median 3.9, IQR 3.0-5.2; 4891 of 4909 corpus spectra surveyed, 2026-07). Distribution is mildly bimodal (secondary mode ~9x), suggesting a second acquisition configuration; per-sample measurement still takes precedence when the continuum permits.
segment_response_fallback.notes: The 365 nm junction is an ADDITIVE baseline artifact (background subtraction's job), not a multiplicative gain, so no fallback ratio is stored for it.
